refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
DP3: Integrate real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding latency constraints to FR and DP to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send communication quality status and packet loss metrics to NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution or Vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but less focused on communication protocol specifics.
Physical vehicle module; communication protocol logic less contained here.
Best containment of communication protocol and packet loss management.
Safety services focus on alerts and policies, not communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Placing the DP in the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
Risk of interface complexity if communication quality data is needed by Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts for telemetry and safety alert data to avoid cross-module diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data may be needed by multiple modules, requiring careful interface management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for DP, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Teleoperation module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module for communication quality.

recommended_revisions: Add latency constraints to FR and DP to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Clarify interface contracts for telemetry and safety alert data to reduce diffusion risks.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirement with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. Verification and containment are feasible with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: To incorporate latency constraints and improve balance between packet loss and teleoperation responsiveness.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with adaptive protocols.
open_risks: Latency impact due to retransmissions may affect teleoperation performance.
Interface complexity with Safety Services for communication quality data.
evidence_needed: Performance testing data under representative network conditions.
Interface usage analysis between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
